problems.text: Define a normal matrix and prove that the following are equivalent.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: \(\|A\|_F=\left(\sum |\lambda_i|^2\right)^{1/2}\), where \(\{\lambda_i\}\) are the eigenvalues of \(A\) counted with multiplicity.

problems.text: Let \(\kappa_2(A)\) be the two-norm condition number of the square, non-singular \(A\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text:
Prove that
\[
\kappa_2(A)=\frac{\sigma_1}{\sigma_m},
\]
where \(\sigma_1\) and \(\sigma_m\) are the largest and smallest singular values of \(A\), respectively.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove or disprove: If \(A=QBQ^*\) with \(Q\) unitary, then \(\kappa_2(A)=\kappa_2(B)\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define the least squares solution to \(Ax=b\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Derive the normal equations for the least squares problem.

subparts.label: (d)
subparts.text: Prove that the unique solution to the least squares problem is \((A^T A)^{-1}A^T b\).

subparts.label: (e)
subparts.text: Describe how to solve the least squares problem using the QR decomposition of \(A\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \(P\) is an orthogonal projector if and only if it is Hermitian.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text:
Let \(\{q_1,q_2,\ldots,q_n\}\) be an orthonormal subset of \(\mathbb{C}^m\). Show that
\[
P=\sum_{i=1}^n q_iq_i^*
\]
is an orthogonal projector with range equal to the span of \(\{q_1,q_2,\ldots,q_n\}\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \(\langle x,y\rangle_A=x^T Ay\) is an inner product on \(\mathbb{R}^m\) if and only if \(A\) is symmetric and positive definite.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Assume now that \(A\) is symmetric and positive definite. If \(x_*\) is the solution to \(Ax=b\) and \(\{p_1,\ldots,p_m\}\) is an orthonormal basis for \(\mathbb{R}^m\) with respect to \(\langle\, ,\,\rangle_A\) and \(x_*=\sum c_i p_i\), give a formula for the \(c_i\).
